symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden-onset expressive/receptive language disturbance (word-finding difficulty, could not understand written words)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute cortical aphasia is a hallmark stroke presentation and can occur in isolation without motor signs.
Word-finding difficulty occurs in post-viral brain fog, but sudden discrete onset is atypical for a chronic flare.
Acute altered cognition is a stroke/seizure/metabolic red flag requiring emergency assessment.
New confusion can be metabolic; requires bedside glucose and bloods.

symptoms.symptom_name: Abrupt onset within minutes after cognitive/screen exertion, not resolving

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sudden onset points to a vascular or ictal event and starts the reperfusion clock.
A discrete cognitive-exertion trigger fits post-exertional symptom exacerbation, but does not exclude a coincident acute event.

symptoms.symptom_name: Background post-viral syndrome with chronic brain fog, memory difficulty and fatiguability

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Establishes the chronic substrate for a post-exertional crash — but is also the anchor that risks under-triage of an acute event.

symptoms.symptom_name: Caregiver-observable FAST motor screen negative — no facial droop, no lateralised limb weakness, no dysarthria

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduces the likelihood of a large-vessel motor stroke but does NOT exclude an isolated cortical aphasic infarct — must not be used to stand down escalation.

symptoms.symptom_name: In-person neurological examination intact and focal — cranial nerves grossly intact, no arm drift, speech mechanically intact, MMSE with intact orientation but impaired serial 7s and delayed recall

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed clinician measurement — the intact focal exam and dysexecutive MMSE pattern are what allow the benign diagnosis; unavailable to the telehealth AI.
An intact hands-on focal neurological exam materially lowers stroke probability — but only a clinician present can obtain it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal vital signs (BP 125/80, HR 88, RR 16, SpO2 99%, Temp 36.8) — clinician-measured

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal observations lower the chance of a gross systemic cause; no home device was reported, so these are sealed clinician measurements, not patient-obtainable.

symptoms.symptom_name: Urgent non-contrast CT brain (+/- CT angiography) and bedside capillary glucose

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Neuroimaging is required to exclude acute infarct/haemorrhage; never available in real time via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency neurological assessment with urgent non-contrast CT brain (and CT angiography per stroke pathway)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Time-critical exclusion of acute ischaemic stroke / haemorrhage in a patient with sudden new aphasia and confusion.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bedside capillary blood glucose and metabolic panel (electrolytes, sodium)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Rapidly reversible metabolic mimics of acute confusion must be excluded.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: 12-lead ECG for atrial fibrillation / embolic source
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Identifies a cardioembolic source if an ischaemic event is confirmed.
